title: Dynamic Key Patterns
description: Pattern definitions for dynamic keys (namespaced IDs, version conditions, etc.)

versionConditionalConfig.description:
Version-conditional configuration pattern.

Allows different configurations for different Minecraft versions.
The `default` key provides the base configuration, and version-conditional
keys override or merge into the default when the runtime version matches.

**Usage:**
```yaml
some-config:
  default:
    # Base configuration (always applied first)
    key1: value1
  $$>=1.21.4:
    # Overrides/merges when version >= 1.21.4
    key1: new_value
  $$1.20.1~1.21.3:
    # Overrides/merges when version is in range
    key2: range_value
```

**Version Condition Formats:**
- `$$>=1.21.4` - Greater than or equal
- `$$<=1.21.3` - Less than or equal
- `$$<1.21.2` - Less than
- `$$=1.21.0` - Exact version
- `$$1.20.1~1.21.3` - Version range (inclusive)

default.description: Default configuration (applied first, then version-specific configs override/merge)

versionConditionalValue.description:
Version-conditional value selection.

Selects different values based on Minecraft version.

**Usage (with default):**
```yaml
client-bound-material:
  default: arrow
  $$>=1.21.2: honey_bottle
```

**Usage (without default):**
```yaml
client-bound-material:
  $$1.20.1~1.21.1: bow
  $$1.21.2~1.21.3: honey_bottle
```

The `default` value is optional and used when no version condition matches.

default.description: Default value (optional, used when no version condition matches)
